LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC") ADEQUATELY PLEADS COMPLIANCE WITH THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT CLAIMS PRESENTATION REQUIREMENTS. WHETHER THE SENIOR CLERK TYPIST WAS AUTHORIZED TO ACCEPT SERVICE ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT PURSUANT TO GOVT. CODE SEC. 915(A) IS A FACTUAL ISSUE THAT GOES BEYOND THE SCOPE OF DEMURRER. THE SAC PLEADS THAT GOVT. CODE SEC. 915(A) WAS COMPLIED WITH. (SAC, 5; DECLARATION OF JOHN C. ELLIS (FILED AS AN ATTACHMENT TO THE SAC), 4.) AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE SAC ADEQUATELY PLEADS FACTS ESTABLISHING THE ELEMENTS OF GOVT. CODE SEC. 815.2. THE SAC PLEADS THAT DEFENDANT AND ITS EMPLOYEES HAD A LEGAL DUTY TO SURROUNDING PROPERTY OWNERS (SAC, 25), THAT AN EMPLOYEE OR AGENT OF DEFENDANT BREACHED THAT DUTY BY GENERATING SPARKS WHILE DOING RENOVATION ON THE BUILDING (SAC, 22), AND THAT THE SPARKS STARTED THE FIRE THAT CAUSED THE PROPERTY DAMAGE. (SAC, 22.) AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE SAC ADEQUATELY PLEADS FACTS ESTABLISHING THE ELEMENTS OF GOVT. CODE SEC. 815.4. THE SAC PLEADS THAT DEFENDANT AND ITS AGENTS HAD A DUTY TO MAINTAIN A SAFE WORKING ENVIRONMENT AT THE CONSTRUCTION SITE, AS WELL AS TO THE ADJACENT PROPERTIES (SAC, 36), BOLLO CONSTRUCTION, AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR OF DEFENDANT, BREACHED ITS DUTY BY GENERATING SPARKS (SAC, 32), AND THE SPARKS CAUSED THE IGNITION OF THE FIRE. (SAC, 32.) AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE SAC DOES NOT ALLEGE A STATUTORY BASIS FOR ITS SUBROGATION CLAIM AGAINST A GOVERNMENT ENTITY. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS 1015 PIERCE STREET INVESTORS LP, RICHARD LENHART, MICHAEL PACIA AND MONTGOMERY PARTNERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST PLAINTIFF S.F.U.S.D. IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSES THAT HIS SON PREVIOUSLY ATTENDED THE CREATIVE ARTS CHARTER SCHOOL BUT WAS NOT AT THE SCHOOL AT THE TIME OF THE FIRE. PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THE DEFENDANTS CAUSED THE FIRE. ALTHOUGH THE CAUSE OF THE FIRE IS UNDETERMINED, SFUSD HAS SUBMITTED EVIDENCE THAT THE FIRE BEGAN IN AN AREA CONTROLLED BY THE DEFENDANTS. (DEPOSITION OF STEVEN ENGLER, 26: 19-23.) SFUSD'S EXPERT OPINED THAT THE SOURCE OF THE FIRE WAS TRASH OR OTHER BURNING OR SMOLDERING MATERIALS AT OR NEAR THE COMBUSTIBLE TRASH CONTAINERS IN THE AREA WHERE THE FIRE BEGAN. (DECLARATION OF JOSEPH ZICHERMAN, PAR.17(C) AND (G).) BASED ON THIS EVIDENCE, A REASONABLE JURY COULD DRAW A REASONABLE INFERENCE THAT THE DEFENDANTS CAUSED THE FIRE. DEFENDANTS' OBJECTION TO THE DECLARATION OF MR. ZICHERMAN IS OVERRULED. MR. ZICHERMAN PROVIDED THE BASES FOR HIS OPINION AND HE MAY RELY ON THE SAN FRANCISCO FIRE DEPARTMENT INVESTIGATION REPORT. NOR HAVE THE DEFENDANTS SHOWN THAT THE RECORD ON THIS MOTION PRECLUDES MR. ZICHERMAN FROM RENDERING HIS OPINIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Against Plaintiff Sfusd
Off Calendar Jun-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
1) Christina M. Locher, Esq. (925-627-3500) of Low McKinley Baleria & Salenko, LLP for Plaintiff San Francisco Unified School Distict.
2) Daniel Schmaeling, Esq. (916-649-8333 x16307) of Law Offices of Harris & Yempuku for Plaintiff Safeco.
3) David Harris, Esq. (213-745-8600) of Tyson & Mendes for Plaintiff San Francisco Unified School District through Court Call.
4) Gary H. Klein, Esq. (818-853-5145) of CP Law Group for Plaintiff Commercial Industrial Building Owners Alliance Inc. dba CIBA through Court Call.
5) Edward Tugade, Esq. (415-757-3488) of Demler Armstrong & Rowland for Defendant Salman Sharriat through Court Call.
6) Sarah Marsey, Esq. (415-546-7500) of Haight for Defendants/Moving party 1015 PIERCE STREET INVESTORS LP, RICHARD LENHART, MICHAEL PACIA and MONTGOMERY PARTNERS, INC.'S

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS 1015 PIERCE STREET INVESTORS LP, RICHARD LENHART, MICHAEL PACIA and MONTGOMERY PARTNERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Against Plaintiff S.F.U.S.D. is DENIED in its entirety. Judge Kahn discloses that his son previously attended the Creative Arts Charter School but was not at the school at the time of the fire. Plaintiff San Francisco Unified School has presented evidence creating a triable issue whether the defendants caused the fire. Although the cause of the fire is undetermined, SFUSD has submitted evidence that the fire began in an area controlled by the defendants. (Deposition of Steven Engler, 26: 19-23.) SFUSD's expert opined that the source of the fire was trash or other burning or smoldering materials at or near the combustible trash containers in the area where the fire began. (Declaration of Joseph Zicherman, par.17(c) and (g).) Based on this evidence, a reasonable jury could draw a reasonable inference that the defendants caused the fire. Defendants' objection to the declaration of Mr. Zicherman is overruled. Mr. Zicherman provided the bases for his opinion and he may rely on the San Francisco Fire Department Investigation Report. Nor have the defendants shown that the record on this motion precludes Mr. Zicherman from rendering his opinions. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/hk)

Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BOLLO CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There is a triable issue whether defendant caused and/or contributed to the spread of the fire. The following evidence creates the triable issue. At pages 179-180 of his deposition, Mr. Lenhart testified that he observed Bollo performing welding work on the school property on the day of the fire. At paragraph 16 of his declaration, Mr. Pickett opines that this hot work could have contributed to the cause and/or spread of the fire. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with Section 437c(g) Code of Civil Procedure and California Rule of Court 3.1312.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. PATRICK J. TORSEY OF CP LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-818-853-5147 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. KEVIN L. PLACE OF THE ARCHER NORRIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-213-437-4000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SFSUD.

RULING - Judge Kahn disclosed that his son goes to the Creative Arts Charter School but was not at the school at the time of the fire. The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant SFUSD's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, for summary adjudication as to the third amended complaint in case 530260 is denied in its entirety. SFUSD failed to maintain its burden of production as to any of the three claims alleged against it. SFUSD fails to show that plaintiffs' claims "cannot be established" within the meaning of CCP 437c(p)(2). The record on this motion, like the record on Mr. Shariat's motion for summary judgment, is equivocal. SFUSD acknowledges that the cause of the fire is undetermined. (UMF 31). Plaintiffs' inability to confirm whether there was welding activity at the construction site on the date of the fire does not establish that plaintiffs are unable to produce evidence that SFUSD, Bolo Construction, its agents, employees, or independent contractors were engaging in other types hot work activities which produced sparks. SFUSD acknowledges that Mr. Lenhart observed hot work being performed at the job site on the date of the fire. (UMF 21.) Even if SFUSD met its burden of production, Mr. Pickett's declaration that the hot work residue could have been fanned by the winds into the tinder dry plant material and wood and that could have caused the fire creates a triable issue whether SFUSD, Bolo Construction, its agents, employees, or independent contractors' caused or contributed to the fire. (Response to UMF 33-35.) Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. EDWARD P. TUGADE OF THE LAW OFFICES OF DEMLER ARMSTRONG & ROWLAND LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-757-3493 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SALMAT SHARIAT;
ATTY. PATRICK J. TORSNEY OF CP LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-818-853-5147 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT 1015 PIERCE STREET INVESTOR BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSED THAT HIS SON GOES TO CREATIVE ARTS SCHOOL BUT WAS NOT AT THE SCHOOL AT THE TIME OF THE FIRE. He does not believe this will create an impact for him not to be fair and impartial. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Salman Shariat's motion for summary judgment as to the third amended complaint in case 530260 is denied. Mr. Shariat failed to maintain his burden of production as to any of the three claims alleged against him. Mr. Shariat fails to show that plaintiffs' claims "cannot be established" within the meaning of CCP 437c(p)(2). The record on this motion is equivocal. In his Fact 42 Mr. Shariat acknowledges that the cause of the fire is undetermined. Mr. Shariat has not submitted any evidence of plaintiffs' factually devoid discovery responses or that plaintiffs necessarily are unable to prove their claims against him. Moreover, the declaration of Mr. Pickett raises a triable issue whether ivy on Mr. Shariat's property was a contributing factor to the cause or spread of the fire. (Pickett Dec. pars. 8 and 13). The objections to those paragraphs of the declaration of Mr. Pickett are overruled. Mr. Pickett's testimony conflicts with the testimony of Mr. Spitulski, who indicates that Mr. Shariat was not a contributing factor or cause of the fire damage. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Feb-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. HELEN S. YI OF THE LAW OFFICES OF ARCHER NORRIS, TEL. NO. (213) 437-4000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SF UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately pleads compliance with the Government Claims Act claims presentation requirements. Whether the senior clerk typist was authorized to accept service on behalf of Defendant pursuant to Govt. Code sec. 915(a) is a factual issue that goes beyond the scope of demurrer. The SAC pleads that Govt. Code sec. 915(a) was complied with. (SAC, 5; Declaration of John C. Ellis (filed as an attachment to the SAC), 4.) As to the second cause of action, the demurrer is overruled. The SAC adequately pleads facts establishing the elements of Govt. Code sec. 815.2. The SAC pleads that Defendant and its employees had a legal duty to surrounding property owners (SAC, 25), that an employee or agent of Defendant breached that duty by generating sparks while doing renovation on the building (SAC, 22), and that the sparks started the fire that caused the property damage. (SAC, 22.) As to the third cause of action, the demurrer is overruled. The SAC adequately pleads facts establishing the elements of Govt. Code sec. 815.4. The SAC pleads that Defendant and its agents had a duty to maintain a safe working environment at the construction site, as well as to the adjacent properties (SAC, 36), Bollo Construction, an independent contractor of Defendant, breached its duty by generating sparks (SAC, 32), and the sparks caused the ignition of the fire. (SAC, 32.) As to the sixth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The SAC does not allege a statutory basis for its subrogation claim against a government entity. (See Hoff v. Vacaville Unified School Dist. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 925, 932; "In the absence of a constitutional requirement, public entities may be held liable only if a statute ... is found declaring them to be liable.") ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
